title: Add Partial Products
description: Add two computed partial products to find the total product.
condition: Expression of the form a + b where a and b are partial products (e.g., 20 + 8).
response: Compute the sum of the two numbers.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Follows place-value and addition principles. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Apply Commutative Property of Multiplication
description: Recognize that changing the order of factors does not change the product.
condition: Multiplication expression of the form a × b or equation a × b = b × ___
response: Rewrite as b × a or fill blank with the other factor
label: Principle / Model

title: Complete Division Equation with Quotient
description: Given a division situation, fill in the dividend, divisor, and quotient to correctly complete the equation.
condition: A word problem or equation of the form a ÷ b = ___ where a and b are given quantities representing equal groups.
response: Compute the quotient a ÷ b and write the correct number in the blank.
label: Principle / Model
example: If Ivan makes 24 balloons and puts them in groups of 3, compute $24 \div 3 = 8$.

title: Decompose a Factor Using Distributive Property
description: Break apart one factor into addends and express the product as a sum of partial products.
condition: Multiplication expression a × b where one factor can be decomposed (e.g., 8 × 3)
response: Rewrite as (m × b) + (n × b) where m + n = a
label: Principle / Model

title: Determine if Expressions Are Equivalent
description: Evaluate expressions and determine whether they are equal to a target expression.
condition: Given a target expression and another expression involving operations.
response: Compute both and state whether they are equal (Yes or No).
label: Concept
example: Is $(4 \times 5) + (4 \times 5)$ equal to $8 \times 5$? Yes, both equal 40.

title: Identify Division Situation
description: Recognize when a word problem represents division (equal groups or measurement).
condition: Word problem describing sharing equally or forming groups of a given size from a total.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Identify Equal Groups Structure
description: Recognize a situation or picture as representing equal groups suitable for multiplication.
condition: A picture or word problem describes objects arranged in equal groups, rows, boxes, or repeated amounts.
response: Interpret the situation as equal groups and prepare to represent it with multiplication.
label: Concept
example: “5 pictures each day for 4 days” is interpreted as equal groups suitable for $5 \times 4$.
notes: Distinguishes multiplicative from additive or subtractive situations.

title: Interpret Array as Multiplication
description: Translate an array with r rows and c columns into a multiplication expression.
condition: Array arranged in r equal rows with c objects in each row.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Links visual model to symbolic multiplication. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Interpret Division as Equal Groups
description: Recognize that division represents splitting a total into equal groups or determining number in each group.
condition: A word problem describing a total being shared equally among a given number of groups or put into equal groups.
response: Represent the situation using a division expression.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Distinguishes division situations from addition, subtraction, or multiplication contexts.

title: Interpret Quotient as Number of Groups
description: Interpret the quotient in a division equation as the number of groups when dividing by group size.
condition: A division equation where the divisor represents the size of each group (e.g., 24 ÷ 3).
response: State that the quotient represents the number of groups formed.
label: Concept
notes: Response is constant interpretation pattern; specific numbers vary in explanation.

title: Match Visual Model to Division Situation
description: Select the picture that correctly represents a given equal-sharing division scenario.
condition: Multiple visual models showing groups and a word problem describing equal sharing of a total among a specific number of groups.
response: Choose the model that shows the correct number of groups and equal number in each group matching the total.
label: Category
example: For 40 pretzels shared among 5 students, select the picture showing 5 equal groups of 8 pretzels.
notes: Requires checking both total (5 × 8 = 40) and equal group size.

title: Represent Array from Rows and Columns
description: Create or interpret an array based on a given number of rows and items in each row.
condition: A specified number of rows and a specified number of items in each row.
response: Draw or recognize an array with that many rows and equal items per row.
label: Production / Skill
example: 3 rows of 4 objects → an array with 3 horizontal rows and 4 objects in each row.

title: Represent Division as Unknown Factor Equation
description: Rewrite a division problem as a multiplication equation with an unknown factor.
condition: Division situation a ÷ b with unknown quotient.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Uses inverse relationship between multiplication and division.

title: Subtract Equal Groups from Total
description: Find the remaining total by subtracting the value of equal groups from an initial total.
condition: Given a total amount and a number of equal groups with known size to remove.
response: Compute remaining amount by subtracting groups × size from total.
label: Principle / Model
example: Started with $36$ juice boxes, $2$ packs of $4$ left: $36 - (2 \times 4) = 36 - 8 = 28$.

title: Translate Word Problem to Multiplication Equation
description: Convert a word problem describing equal groups into a multiplication equation.
condition: A word problem states a number of groups and a number in each group.
response: Write an equation of the form groups × number_in_each = total.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires mapping language ('rows of', 'each', 'for ___ days') to factors.

title: Update Quantity After Increase
description: Add an additional amount to an existing quantity to find the new total.
condition: An initial quantity q is increased by an additional amount k.
response: Compute q + k to find the new quantity.
label: Principle / Model
example: Ivan has 6 red beads and gets 4 more → $6 + 4 = 10$.

title: Write Division Equation from Context
description: Translate an equal-groups word problem into a complete division equation with dividend, divisor, and quotient.
condition: A word problem giving a total and either number of groups or size of each group.
response: Write an equation of the form total ÷ groups = number in each group (or total ÷ number in each group = groups).
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Structure depends on what quantity is unknown.

title: Write Multiplication Equation from Picture
description: Form a multiplication equation based on a visual model of equal groups.
condition: A picture shows a specific number of equal groups with the same number of objects in each group.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires counting groups and items per group before forming equation.
